name: American Express Membership Rewards Credit Card

fees.waiverCondition: Spend ₹1,50,000 in the previous year to earn 4,500 bonus MR points (worth more than fee)

categoryRates.description: 18 MR points on every ₹200 spent at select partner merchants
1 MR point per ₹50 spent on all other purchases

welcomeBenefits.description: 4,000 bonus MR points on spending ₹20,000 in first 90 days
welcomeBenefits.condition: Spend ₹20,000 within 90 days of card issuance

additionalBenefits: 18 MR on ₹200 at select partners including dining and shopping
Amex Offers - rotating deals with statement credits
MR points transferable to airline miles (Delta SkyMiles, etc.)
pros: Excellent accelerated rewards at partner merchants (18 MR/₹200)
MR points transferable to airline miles for higher value
Amex Offers provide regular statement credits
Good milestone benefits that offset annual fee
cons: Base earn rate of 1 MR/₹50 is modest
Amex acceptance limited at some Indian merchants
Point value varies widely based on redemption method
idealFor: Users who frequently shop at Amex partner merchants
Those who want to accumulate airline miles through credit card spend

faqs.question: What are the select partner merchants?
faqs.answer: Partners rotate but typically include popular dining chains, department stores, and online platforms. Check the Amex India website for the current partner list.

faqs.question: Can I transfer MR points to airlines?
faqs.answer: Yes, MRCC points can be transferred to airline partners like Delta SkyMiles, though the transfer ratios vary. Check the MR portal for current options.

faqs.question: How do Amex Offers work?
faqs.answer: Amex Offers are personalized deals that appear in your Amex app. When you enroll in an offer and shop at that merchant with your card, you get a statement credit or bonus MR points automatically.

faqs.question: Is the annual fee effectively free?
faqs.answer: While the ₹1,000 fee isn't directly waived, spending ₹1,50,000 earns you 4,500 bonus MR points (worth ₹1,350+), effectively more than covering the fee.
